topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink swam on the page, a damning indictment of my efforts. A C- in Advanced Calculus. It felt like a physical weight settling on my chest, making each breath shallow. I stared at the score, my gaze snagging on the abysmal number, and a hot flush crept up my neck. My hands began to tremble, the pen I held suddenly slick with sweat. I slowly lowered the paper onto the desk, the small sound echoing loudly in the quiet room. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and just let my head fall against the cool surface of the desk. The silence that followed was thick, suffocating.

[story 2]

I reread the feedback, the professor’s marginalia a series of sharp, unforgiving critiques. Every sentence was a tiny needle pricking at my composure. My stomach twisted into a tight knot, a nauseating churn that made me want to physically recoil from the desk. I picked up my pencil, but my fingers felt clumsy, disconnected from my will. I chewed on the end of it, the dry wood tasting like ash. Looking out the window, the afternoon sun seemed offensively bright, illuminating the dust motes dancing in the air—a stark contrast to the dull ache blooming in my chest. I sighed, a long, ragged expulsion of air that carried none of my usual confidence.

[story 3]

When the results were posted, the world seemed to tilt on its axis. I walked to my locker, the metal cold against my fingertips, and stared blankly at my reflection. My face looked slack, my eyes shadowed with a deep, weary disappointment. A wave of profound deflation washed over me, leaving me feeling strangely hollowed out. I didn't bother to open my textbook; the weight of the material suddenly seemed insurmountable. I pulled out my phone, scrolling aimlessly through social media, the bright, curated happiness of others feeling like a deliberate mockery. I just wanted to disappear into the fabric of the hallway, to become invisible.
